2603.18656.title: Balanced Thinking: Improving Chain of Thought Training in Vision Language Models
2603.18656.paper_summary: Multimodal reasoning in vision-language models (VLMs) typically relies on a two-stage process: supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning (RL). In standard SFT, all tokens contribute equally to the loss, even though reasoning data are inherently token-imbalanced. Long <think> traces overshadow short but task-critical <answer> segments, leading to verbose reasoning and inaccurate answers. We propose SCALe (Scheduled Curriculum Adaptive Loss), which explicitly separates supervision over reasoning and answer segments using dynamic, length-independent weighting. Unlike vanilla SFT, which overweights the <think> segment, SCALe-SFT gradually shifts the focus from <think> to <answer> throughout training via a cosine scheduling policy, encouraging concise and well-grounded reasoning. We evaluate SCALe across diverse benchmarks and architectures. Results show that SCALe consistently improves accuracy over vanilla SFT and matches the performance of the full two-phase SFT + GRPO pipeline while requiring only about one-seventh of the training time, making it a lightweight yet effective alternative. When combined with GRPO, SCALe achieves the best overall performance, highlighting its value both as a standalone method and as a strong foundation for reinforcement refinement.
2603.18656.paper_summary_zh: 多模態推理在視覺-語言模型（VLMs）中通常依賴於兩階段過程：監督微調（SFT）和強化學習（RL）。在標準的 SFT 中，所有標記對損失的貢獻是相等的，即使推理數據本質上是標記不平衡的。長的 <think> 跡線掩蓋了短但對任務至關重要的 <answer> 片段，導致冗長的推理和不準確的答案。我們提出了 SCALe（定期課程自適應損失），它明確地使用動態、與長度無關的加權來分離對推理和答案片段的監督。與普通的 SFT 不同，SCALe-SFT 在訓練過程中通過餘弦調度策略逐漸將重點從 <think> 轉移到 <answer>，鼓勵簡潔且有根據的推理。我們在多樣的基準和架構上評估 SCALe。結果顯示，SCALe 始終在準確性上超越普通 SFT，並且與完整的兩階段 SFT + GRPO 流程的表現相匹配，同時只需約七分之一的訓練時間，使其成為一種輕量且有效的替代方案。當與 GRPO 結合時，SCALe 實現了最佳的整體性能，突顯了它作為獨立方法的價值以及作為強大強化精煉基礎的潛力。
2603.18656.author: Shaked Perek et.al.
2603.18656.authors: Shaked Perek, Ben Wiesel, Avihu Dekel, Nimrod Shabtay, Eli Schwartz
